content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Accept the amendment: the current legacy backfill collapses implicit canonical slot ids and explicit ids, creating a real backward-compatibility regression when canonical plan.json files lose explicit_id on round-trip.

## Votes

- `fr-8-aacc0cb9`: Accept
  This is a real correctness bug. render_plan_json canonicalizes every proposal to include bead_id, while explicit_id is the field that preserves whether that id was planner-supplied or just the implicit slot. In src/cli/project.rs:586-595, backfill_legacy_explicit_bead_flags erases that distinction by treating any missing-flag proposal with a bead_id as explicit. resolve_bead_plan then takes the id-match path in src/cli/project.rs:842-852, so a round-tripped implicit proposal with a renamed live bead is incorrectly treated as confirmed and inherits flow_override, plan_hash, and plan_version. The existing legacy-plan test in tests/cli.rs:2029-2127 shows this compatibility path is intended; it just does not cover title drift. A regression test for stripped explicit_id plus renamed live bead is warranted, and the loader needs a discriminator that preserves implicit canonical slot ids in that legacy round-trip case.
